name: Ocaña and Noblejas Reservoir Loop
name_original: Laguna de Noblejas – circular desde Ocaña
description: This moderate 15.6-mile touring cycling route from Ocaña explores La Mancha's fields, the Noblejas Reservoir, and historic towns.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Ocaña offers routes through the plains of Castilla-La Mancha, characterized by open agricultural landscapes and scattered woodlands. The region features gentle undulations, making it suitable for various cycling abilities. Routes often connect historical towns and natural features like reservoirs and pine forests. The terrain provides a mix of paved roads and well-maintained tracks, ideal for touring bicycles. Best touring cycling routes around Ocaña The most popular touring cycling route is Royal Palace of Aranjuez and the Prince's Garden – circular from Ocaña, a 23.4 miles (37.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 0 minutes to complete. This route explores historical gardens and royal architecture. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ocaña and Noblejas Reservoir Loop, a moderate 15.6 miles (25.2 km) path. This trail offers views of the Noblejas Reservoir and surrounding open countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Pinares de Yepes – Toledo Gate of Yepes loop from Yepes, a 29.7 miles (47.8 km) trail leading through pine forests and past historical gates, often completed in about 4 hours 39 minutes. Touring cycling around Ocaña is defined by open plains, pine forests, and historical townscapes, with options for both beginner touring bike routes Ocaña and more advanced bike trails near Ocaña. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Ocaña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ocaña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Ocaña?
A: There are over 75 touring cycling routes around Ocaña, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include options for various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging excursions through the region's varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Ocaña?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ocaña traverse the plains of Castilla-La Mancha, characterized by open agricultural landscapes and scattered woodlands. You'll encounter gentle undulations, with a mix of paved roads and well-maintained tracks, making it suitable for touring bicycles.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Ocaña?
A: Yes, Ocaña offers 14 easy touring cycling routes. The region's gentle undulations and well-maintained tracks make it a good destination for those new to touring cycling or looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes near Ocaña?
A: For more experienced touring cyclists, there are 18 difficult routes around Ocaña. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19444761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinares de Yepes – Toledo Gate of Yepes loop from Yepes&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 29.7 miles (47.8 km) and features significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Ocaña?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Ocaña are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022692051/royal-palace-of-aranjuez-and-the-prince-s-garden-circular-from-ocana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Aranjuez and the Prince's Garden – circular from Ocaña&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 23.4-mile (37.7 km) loop exploring historical gardens and architecture.

Q: What historical sites or natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Ocaña's touring cycling routes often connect historical towns and natural features. You can explore highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4576777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4184029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jardín del Príncipe&lt;/a&gt;, and various lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5225122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Seca de las Esteras&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/407021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinescos Pond&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022588683/ocana-and-noblejas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ocaña and Noblejas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; also offers views of the Noblejas Reservoir.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Ocaña?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Ocaña is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder temperatures of winter.

Q: Are there routes that pass by reservoirs or water bodies?
A: Yes, several routes offer views of water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022588683/ocana-and-noblejas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ocaña and Noblejas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice that provides scenic views of the Noblejas Reservoir and the surrounding open countryside.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Ocaña?
A: The touring cycling routes around Ocaña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's open plains, pine forests, and the opportunity to explore historical townscapes and natural features like reservoirs.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the pine forests of the region?
A: Absolutely. The region features scattered woodlands and pine forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19444761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinares de Yepes – Toledo Gate of Yepes loop from Yepes&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, leading you through beautiful pine forests and past historical gates.

Q: Are there any routes that start or pass through Aranjuez?
A: Yes, Aranjuez is a significant point of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022692051/royal-palace-of-aranjuez-and-the-prince-s-garden-circular-from-ocana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Aranjuez and the Prince's Garden – circular from Ocaña&lt;/a&gt; is a popular route that explores the historical gardens and royal architecture of Aranjuez. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32005370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa del Labrador – Glorieta de la Arquitectura loop from Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: The Tower of the Church of San Martín belonged to the disappeared church of the same name; It is in the Renaissance-Herrerian style and its construction began in 1562 and was completed in 1577. The works were directed by D. Francisco Sánchez and it was carried out by the brothers Lucas and Pedro de la Villa.

Ocaña stands on the plateau in its own territory, La Mesa de Ocaña, which elevates it with landscapes, monuments, culture and history. Portico de la Mancha, its urban fabric treasures religious and civil legacies.

The historic center of Noblejas (Toledo) combines tradition, religious architecture, and cultural spaces with modern corners.
Church of Santiago Apóstol

Herrerian and neoclassical style, built in the 17th century.
It has three naves and six chapels, with ornamentation from Toledo workshops.
Hermitage of Santa María Magdalena

Located in Plaza Manuel García Oliva.
Baroque in style, with a Latin cross layout and a tower at its foot.
Plaza de José Bono

Modern arcaded plaza (2000-2006) that contrasts with the traditional architecture.
Meeting point and institutional space.
Other nearby attractions

Bodega Grande: an old 19th-century winery, a symbol of the winemaking tradition.
Cueva de Villacampa: a Roman gypsum (lapis specularis) mine, accessible only with a guide.

Modern arcaded plaza (2000-2006) that contrasts with the traditional architecture.
Meeting point and institutional space.

The Noblejas reservoir is a small pool of water for irrigation in the Fuente Berrato stream. Its waters are home to different species of flora and fauna and create an oasis in the dry landscape of olive groves.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Laguna de Noblejas circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 25.2 km long and typically takes around 1 hour and 54 minutes to complete by touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Laguna de Noblejas circular route is described as 'very simple' and 'suitable for the whole family'. It primarily traverses dirt roads and agricultural areas, making it an accessible and enjoyable experience for most cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect to cycle primarily on dirt roads through vast cultivated fields and agricultural landscapes. The terrain is generally flat, offering a smooth ride through the picturesque La Mancha region.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Laguna de Noblejas circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is particularly recommended during spring and autumn. During these seasons, the landscape is at its most vibrant, offering beautiful colors and pleasant temperatures for cycling.

faqs.question: What historical and cultural sights can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Starting from Ocaña, you can explore significant monuments like the Plaza Mayor de Ocaña and the Tower and Portal of San Martín. As you reach Noblejas, you'll find the Noblejas Historic Center, known as the 'wine capital of Mesa de Ocaña', and the Noblejas Reservoir.

faqs.question: What natural features or wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through agricultural landscapes, offering opportunities to observe local fauna and the changing colors of the cultivated fields depending on the season. You'll also pass by the Noblejas Reservoir, a notable natural feature.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna de Noblejas circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is generally accessible, specific regulations regarding dogs on cycling trails can vary. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife, and to ensure they are comfortable with the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Laguna de Noblejas circular route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I find parking near the starting point in Ocaña?
faqs.answer: Ocaña, being the starting point, offers various parking options within the town. You can typically find parking near the historic center or other public areas before beginning your ride.

faqs.question: How can I get to Ocaña using public transport with my bike?
faqs.answer: Ocaña is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services from larger cities like Madrid. It's advisable to check with the specific bus company regarding their policy on transporting bicycles, as this can vary and may require special arrangements or bike bags.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Both Ocaña and Noblejas offer amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel. Noblejas is known as the 'wine capital of Mesa de Ocaña,' providing opportunities to experience local gastronomy.

waypoints.name: Tower and Portal of San Martín
